messages.content:
Barkha Dutt is in the habit of prefacing her comments on Shah Faesal saying he topped the IAS examinations . So what? Every year someone tops. That is no guarantee of good politics.
Or you find some social media persons saying such and such person is a Professor of Harvard or LSE. So what? The opinion the person expresses is not that of the University he teaches in. It is an expression of his own political belief or that of the party he belongs to.
Just take the case of Subramanian Swamy. He had a brilliant academic career. He did his PhD in Economics under the Nobel Laureate Simon Kuznets and taught in some of the most prestigious educational institutions including Harvard. Does that mean we have to accept everything he says as gospel truth?
Also look at his expertise. If he is an economist then we will listen carefully on what he speaks on economics. If he talks on astrology or which prawn cutlet is best in Calcutta-Allen's or Mitra's that is a subject on which he is like you and me. Rabindranath liked Poyodhi of Jalajog. Fine. But I like Ganguram's and that is no disrespect to Tagore.
Examine what a person is saying not where he studied or teaches.
